metadata.title: Case studies - success stories
metadata.description: Discover how companies achieve global expansion success with Jackson & Frank's EOR, payroll, and global mobility services.

hero.title: Real success stories
hero.description: Discover how businesses achieve global expansion success with our comprehensive HR solutions.

paragraphs: The case studies below show how companies like yours use Jackson & Frank to expand into new countries without setting up local entities. From retail and automotive to technology and manufacturing, our clients rely on our Employer of Record (EOR), payroll, and global mobility services to hire quickly and scale across 80+ countries.

Each story outlines the business challenge, the solution we implemented, and the results achieved including time to hire, cost savings, and compliance outcomes. Whether you're planning your first international hire or scaling an existing global team, these examples illustrate what's possible with the right partner.

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions about our case studies
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about our success stories and how we work with clients

items.question: Can you help with a similar expansion to the ones in these case studies?
items.answer: Yes. Our Employer of Record (EOR), payroll, and mobility services are available in 80+ countries. Share your target countries and timeline via the contact form or book a call, and we'll outline a tailored approach and proposal.

items.question: How long does it typically take to go live in a new country?
items.answer: In most cases we can have employees onboarded within days to a few weeks, depending on the country and role. The case studies show real timelines; we'll give you a specific estimate for your situation.

items.question: Do you work with companies in my industry?
items.answer: We work across industries including retail, technology, automotive, professional services, and more. The case studies reflect a sample; we can discuss your industry and use cases in a consultation.

items.question: How do I get a custom proposal for my expansion?
items.answer: Use the contact form or the Get started button below. Tell us your target countries, headcount, and timeline. Our team will respond with a tailored proposal and next steps, usually within 24 hours.

items.question: Are the results in case studies typical?
items.answer: Results reflect real client outcomes. Timelines and savings vary by country, headcount, and scope. We'll give you a realistic estimate for your situation when you reach out.

items.question: Do you work with startups as well as enterprises?
items.answer: Yes. We work with companies of all sizes from startups hiring their first international employee to enterprises scaling across many countries. Our solutions scale with your needs.

items.question: Can I speak with a reference or similar client?
items.answer: We can sometimes arrange reference calls with clients in similar industries or regions, subject to confidentiality. Ask your account manager when we discuss your expansion plan.

items.question: What services are included in your case study engagements?
items.answer: Case studies often include Employer of Record (EOR), payroll, compliance, immigration, and onboarding. Exact scope depends on the client; we'll tailor services to your expansion goals when you contact us.

caseStudies.title: Enabling fast retail store launch in Rome
caseStudies.excerpt: A global retail brand launched its first store in Rome with 24 employees hired and onboarded in 20 days zero delays, no local entity required.
caseStudies.testimonial: We needed a full team in Rome in weeks with no entity and no local HR. Jackson & Frank made it simple they were our legal employer and took care of hiring, contracts, and payroll. We opened on time and could focus entirely on the store and our customers.

background.title: Overview
background.content: A global retail and lifestyle brand set out to open its first store in Rome. The company had no legal entity or HR presence in Italy and needed a full store team in place in weeks to meet their launch date.

problem.title: Client challenge
problem.content: Retail openings depend on having a full team ready on day one. The client had no entity in Italy, no local HR or recruitment setup, and limited knowledge of Italian employment law. Hiring a full store team with proper contracts, payroll, and benefits in time for the opening was at risk any delay would push back the launch and hurt market entry.

solution.title: Our solution
solution.content: We acted as the legal employer in Italy and delivered end-to-end workforce setup: recruitment coordination, proper contracts and documentation, payroll and benefits, and structured onboarding so the client could open on schedule without establishing a local entity.

results.label: Employees hired
results.description: Retail team onboarded in 20 days.

results.label: Days to workforce
results.description: Full store team operational in under three weeks.

results.label: Compliance risks
results.description: Italian labor and payroll rules strictly adhered to.

results.label: Launch delays
results.description: Store launched on time; no entity setup required.

fullContent.executiveSummary: A global retail and lifestyle brand wanted to open its first store in Rome. With no entity or HR in [Italy](/italy), they needed a full local team in place within weeks to hit their launch date. We provided [Employer of Record](/employer-of-record) and [payroll](/payroll) so they could hire, onboard, and manage staff properly and open on schedule.

businessChallenge.intro: Retail openings require a full team from day one. The client faced:
bullets: No legal entity or HR infrastructure in [[Italy]]
No in-house knowledge of Italian employment law
Need to hire a full store team in weeks with proper [[payroll]], contracts, and benefits
Fixed store opening date delays would delay market entry
businessChallenge.closing: They needed a single partner to act as legal employer and run all workforce operations.

fullContent.solutionIntro: We delivered a fully managed [EOR](/employer-of-record) solution aligned to their launch timeline.

keyServices.title: 1. Local hiring and workforce setup
items: Offers and contracts under Italian law
Statutory documentation and labor law adherence

keyServices.title: 2. Payroll and benefits
items: Monthly [[payroll]], tax and social security, benefits

keyServices.title: 3. Onboarding and ongoing HR
items: HR policies and employee lifecycle management

implementationApproach.intro: We ran a clear, time-bound process:
steps: Week 1: Requirements and Italian compliance review
Weeks 1–2: Recruitment coordination and candidate selection
Weeks 2–3: Contracting, documentation, and payroll setup
Week 3: Onboarding and go-live support
implementationApproach.closing: This kept the launch on track with proper legal adherence.

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions about retail store launch in Rome
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about hiring and opening a retail store in Italy with EOR

items.question: How fast can I hire retail staff in Italy without a local entity?
items.answer: With an [[Employer of Record]] ([EOR](/employer-of-record)) in [Italy](/italy), you can typically hire and onboard a full store team within 2–3 weeks. In this [case study](/case-studies), 24 retail employees were hired and onboarded in 20 days with no local entity, so the store could open on schedule.

items.question: What does an EOR handle when opening a retail store in Rome?
items.answer: An [[EOR]] acts as the legal employer and typically handles recruitment coordination, employment contracts under [Italian law](/italy), payroll and benefits, tax and social security, and onboarding so you can focus on store operations and customer experience without setting up a local entity.

items.question: Do I need a legal entity in Italy to open a retail store?
items.answer: No. Using an [[Employer of Record]] in [Italy](/italy), you can open a store and employ staff without establishing your own Italian entity. The EOR is the legal employer and ensures [compliance](/compliance) with Italian labor and payroll regulations.

items.question: What are the compliance risks of hiring retail employees in Italy?
items.answer: [Italian](/italy) labor law covers contracts, working hours, benefits, and [payroll](/payroll). An [[EOR]] ensures proper contracts, statutory documentation, and adherence to local rules, reducing [[compliance]] risk so you can launch without delays or penalties.

items.question: Can an EOR help with recruitment for a retail store in Italy?
items.answer: Yes. Many [[EOR]] providers, including in this [case study](/case-studies), offer recruitment coordination and candidate support alongside employment and payroll so you get a full store team ready for opening day without managing multiple vendors.

caseStudies.title: Automotive industry success story
caseStudies.excerpt: A leading EV manufacturer relocated 117 employees across 5 countries in 8 months with on-time product launch and 15% lower relocation costs.
caseStudies.testimonial: Moving 117 people across five countries could have derailed our launch. Jackson & Frank ran the whole process visas, [[compliance]], onboarding so we could stay focused on product and market. We hit our deadlines and kept legal risk under control.

background.title: Overview
background.content: A leading electric vehicle manufacturer was scaling into new European markets and needed to move a large number of employees across borders. They required a partner to handle relocation, work permits, and compliance so they could meet production and launch timelines.

problem.title: Client challenge
problem.content: Cross-border moves were straining internal HR: visa and work permit complexity, differing labor laws, and slow relocation were threatening production schedules and market delivery. Delays would directly impact product launch and demand fulfillment.

solution.title: Our solution
solution.content: We delivered end-to-end global mobility: work permits and visas, regulatory support, and relocation assistance. We relocated 117 employees across five countries in 8 months so the client could deploy talent on time in every jurisdiction.

results.label: Employees relocated
results.description: Relocated across 5 countries within 8 months, all deadlines met.

results.label: Countries covered
results.description: Local labor laws adhered to across all jurisdictions.

results.label: Product launch
results.description: Relocation delays removed; products launched on schedule.

results.label: Cost reduction
results.description: Relocation costs reduced by 15%; better operational efficiency.

fullContent.executiveSummary: A leading EV manufacturer was expanding into new European markets and needed to relocate a large workforce across borders. With no single partner for visas, relocation, and regulatory requirements, timelines and legal risk were at stake. We provided end-to-end global mobility and relocated 117 employees across five countries in 8 months with on-time product launch.

businessChallenge.intro: Managing cross-border moves at scale was the main bottleneck:
bullets: Complex visa and work permit processes in multiple countries
Varying local labor laws and [[compliance]] requirements
Relocation timing affecting production and product launch
Need to align talent deployment with market demand
businessChallenge.closing: Without a dedicated mobility partner, delays and [[compliance]] gaps would put launch and operations at risk.

fullContent.solutionIntro: We provided end-to-end global mobility aligned with the client's expansion and launch timeline.

keyServices.title: 1. Immigration and work authorization
items: Work permit and visa handling across five countries

keyServices.title: 2. Compliance and local labor law
items: Local employment and labor rules properly followed

implementationApproach.intro: We ran a phased, country-by-country rollout:
steps: Months 1–2: Country assessment, eligibility, and immigration planning
Months 2–5: Visa and work permit processing by country
Months 4–7: Relocation execution and local onboarding
Months 6–8: Stabilization, compliance checks, and handover
implementationApproach.closing: This kept all moves on schedule and properly managed.

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions about automotive global mobility and relocation
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about relocating employees across multiple countries for automotive and EV

items.question: How do you relocate a large workforce across multiple countries for product launch?
items.answer: End-to-end [global hiring](/global-hiring-guide) mobility covers work permits and visas, [compliance](/compliance) with local labor laws, and relocation coordination. In this [case study](/case-studies), 117 employees were relocated across five countries in 8 months with on-time product launch and 15% lower relocation costs.

items.question: What countries can you support for automotive industry relocation?
items.answer: We support relocation and employment in 80+ countries. This [case study](/case-studies) involved [Germany](/germany), [Netherlands](/netherlands), [Poland](/poland), [Czech Republic](/czech-republic), and [France](/france) typical markets for EV and automotive expansion in Europe.

items.question: Can global mobility reduce relocation costs?
items.answer: Yes. Centralizing visas, [compliance](/compliance), and relocation with one partner can reduce costs and streamline timelines. In this EV manufacturer [case study](/case-studies), relocation costs were reduced by 15% while meeting all deadlines.

items.question: Who handles visas and work permits for cross-border moves?
items.answer: A global mobility partner typically handles work permit and visa applications, documentation, and government liaison across each country, so your HR team can focus on talent and operations instead of immigration paperwork.

items.question: How long does it take to relocate employees to multiple European countries?
items.answer: Timelines depend on country and headcount. In this case, 117 employees were relocated across five countries in 8 months with phased rollout country assessment, visa processing, relocation execution, and stabilization keeping product launch on schedule.

caseStudies.title: Expanding overseas operations for a German SaaS scale up
caseStudies.excerpt: A German SaaS scale up onboarded 12 engineers across 3 countries in 3 months with 90% fewer [[payroll]] errors.
caseStudies.testimonial: We needed to hire engineers in three countries without setting up entities. Jackson & Frank made it straightforward [[EOR]], multi-country [[payroll]], and [[compliance]] in one place. We grew the team fast and cut [[payroll]] errors significantly.

background.title: Overview
background.content: A fast-growing German SaaS company wanted to scale its engineering team across Europe for localization and product growth. They needed to hire in multiple countries without setting up local entities and without getting bogged down in legal and [[payroll]] complexity.

problem.title: Client challenge
problem.content: Hiring engineers abroad meant dealing with different employment laws, tax and [[payroll]] rules in each country. In-house [[payroll]] was error-prone and expansion was slowing down. The company needed a way to hire and pay people in several countries quickly.

solution.title: Our solution
solution.content: We acted as EOR in three countries and ran hiring, contracts, and multi-country [[payroll]]. We provided localized contracts and benefits and ensured full legal and tax compliance so the client could focus on product and growth.

results.label: Engineers onboarded
results.description: 12 engineers onboarded across 3 countries in 3 months.

results.label: Penalties
results.description: Legal requirements met in all three countries.

results.label: Payroll error reduction
results.description: Payroll errors reduced by 90%; smoother operations.

fullContent.executiveSummary: A German SaaS scale up needed to grow its engineering team across Europe without opening local entities. Legal and [payroll](/payroll) complexity was slowing them down. We provided [EOR](/employer-of-record) and multi-country payroll, onboarded 12 engineers in three countries in 3 months with a 90% drop in payroll errors.

businessChallenge.intro: Expanding the engineering team across borders created real friction:
bullets: Hiring skilled engineers in multiple countries under different employment laws
Complex legal and tax [[compliance]] for cross-border employment
[[Payroll]] and benefits across jurisdictions error prone when done in-house
Expansion speed limited by admin and [[compliance]] burden
businessChallenge.closing: They needed one partner to handle employment, [payroll](/payroll), and [compliance](/compliance) so they could scale without delay.

fullContent.solutionIntro: We delivered EOR and integrated multi-country payroll tailored to their expansion.

keyServices.title: 1. EOR and hiring
items: Legal employer in Germany, Netherlands, and Poland

keyServices.title: 3. Compliance
items: Local labor and tax rules properly followed

steps: Month 1: Country compliance review and EOR setup in each jurisdiction
Months 1–2: Hiring, contracting, and payroll configuration
Months 2–3: Onboarding, go-live payroll, and ongoing support
implementationApproach.closing: This delivered a scalable setup in three months.

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions about SaaS scale up hiring in Europe
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about hiring engineers across multiple countries without entities

items.question: Can I hire engineers in multiple European countries without local entities?
items.answer: Yes. An [[Employer of Record]] ([EOR](/employer-of-record)) can act as the legal employer in each country. In this [case study](/case-studies), a German SaaS scale-up onboarded 12 engineers across [Germany](/germany), [Netherlands](/netherlands), and [Poland](/poland) in 3 months with no local entities and 90% fewer payroll errors.

items.question: How does multi-country payroll work for a scale up?
items.answer: An [[EOR]] runs unified [payroll](/payroll) across each country local contracts, tax and social security, and benefits from one place. This reduces errors and admin burden so you can scale the team without building in-house payroll in every jurisdiction.

items.question: What compliance is needed when hiring in Germany, Netherlands, and Poland?
items.answer: Each country has its own labor and tax rules. An [[EOR]] ensures localized contracts, statutory requirements, and ongoing regulatory [compliance](/compliance) in all three, so you avoid penalties and focus on product and growth.

items.question: How long does it take to hire engineers in Europe with an EOR?
items.answer: Setup typically takes a few weeks; hiring and onboarding depend on roles and locations. In this case, 12 engineers were onboarded across three countries in 3 months with zero [[compliance]] penalties.

items.question: Can an EOR reduce payroll errors for international teams?
items.answer: Yes. Centralized multi-country [payroll](/payroll) with local expertise can significantly reduce errors. In this SaaS [case study](/case-studies), [[payroll]] errors were reduced by 90% while scaling the engineering team across three countries.

caseStudies.title: Supporting Global Growth for a Chinese Solar Manufacturer
caseStudies.excerpt: A Chinese solar manufacturer added 50 employees across multiple markets in 5 months and relocated 10+ engineers with on schedule delivery.
caseStudies.testimonial: Scaling into new markets while moving engineers was overwhelming. Jackson & Frank took care of visas, relocation, and regulatory requirements so we could keep projects on track and grow the team where it mattered.

background.title: Overview
background.content: A leading Chinese solar panel manufacturer was scaling to meet global demand and needed to move engineers to new facilities abroad and grow local teams in several countries. They required support with visas, relocation, and labor and tax compliance across borders.

problem.title: Client challenge
problem.content: Scaling meant visa and relocation complexity for engineers and hiring in new markets each with different labor and tax rules. Delays or compliance gaps would push project timelines and increase legal and financial risk.

solution.title: Our solution
solution.content: We managed visa and relocation for engineers and provided compliance and legal support across markets. We ran compliance reviews and workforce planning so the client could scale safely and keep projects on schedule.

results.label: Employees added
results.description: 50 employees added across multiple international markets in 5 months.

results.label: Legal risks
results.description: Financial and regulatory requirements met in all markets.

results.label: Engineers relocated
results.description: 10+ engineers relocated; projects delivered on time.

fullContent.executiveSummary: A Chinese solar panel manufacturer was scaling globally and needed to relocate engineers and grow teams in multiple countries. Visa and regulatory complexity threatened timelines. We delivered [immigration](/immigration), relocation, and regulatory support adding 50 employees and relocating 10+ engineers in 5 months.

businessChallenge.intro: Global scaling brought three pressure points:

Labor and tax [[compliance]] across multiple countries

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions about solar manufacturer global expansion
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about visas, relocation, and compliance for renewable energy scaling

items.question: How do you relocate engineers for a solar or renewable energy company expanding globally?
items.answer: A global mobility and compliance partner can handle visa and work permit processing, relocation coordination, and regulatory support. In this case, 10+ engineers were relocated and 50 employees added across multiple markets in 5 months with on schedule delivery.

items.question: What compliance is needed when scaling a manufacturing presence across countries?
items.answer: Labor and tax rules vary by country. We provide compliance reviews, regulatory audits, and ongoing alignment so you can scale workforce and projects without legal or financial risk as in this solar manufacturer expansion.

items.question: Can you support workforce planning for renewable energy companies?
items.answer: Yes. Services can include local hiring support in key markets, workforce structure advice, and retention alongside visa, relocation, and compliance so projects stay on track while you grow the team where it matters.

items.question: Which markets do you support for solar and renewable energy expansion?
items.answer: We support 80+ countries. This [case study](/case-studies) involved [China](/china), [Germany](/germany), [Netherlands](/netherlands), [Poland](/poland), and [Spain](/spain) common markets for solar panel manufacturing and global demand with visas, relocation, and [compliance](/compliance) handled in each.

items.question: How long does it take to add employees and relocate engineers in multiple markets?
items.answer: Timelines depend on countries and headcount. In this Chinese solar manufacturer case, 50 employees were added and 10+ engineers relocated across multiple markets in 5 months with zero legal risks and on schedule project delivery.

caseStudies.title: Automotive Company Global Relocation China to the Netherlands
caseStudies.excerpt: Rapid relocation of 35 employees from [[China]] to the [[Netherlands]] in 2 months no delays.
caseStudies.testimonial: We had to get 35 people from [[China]] to the [[Netherlands]] in two months. Jackson & Frank took care of visas, employment, and [[payroll]] so we could focus on engineering and delivery. No [[compliance]] issues, no delays exactly what we needed.

problem.title: Client challenge

solution.title: Our solution

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions about relocating employees from China to the Netherlands
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about EOR, visas, and payroll for China–Netherlands relocation

items.question: How fast can you relocate employees from China to the Netherlands?
items.answer: With an integrated [[EOR]] and [immigration](/immigration) program, relocation can be completed in as little as 2 months. In this [case study](/case-studies), 35 employees were relocated from [China](/china) to the [Netherlands](/netherlands) in 2 months with all work permits secured and zero deployment delays.

items.question: Do I need a Dutch entity to employ people relocated from China to the Netherlands?
items.answer: No. An [[Employer of Record]] ([EOR](/employer-of-record)) in the [Netherlands](/netherlands) can act as the legal employer handling contracts, [payroll](/payroll), and compliance so you can deploy talent without establishing a Dutch entity.

items.question: What is involved in relocating staff from China to the Netherlands?
items.answer: Typically: [immigration](/immigration) and work permit applications, [[EOR]] employment and contracts, Dutch [payroll](/payroll) and tax setup, and relocation and onboarding support. One partner can manage the full flow so your team can focus on the European project.

items.question: Are Dutch work permits difficult to obtain for international relocation?
items.answer: Dutch immigration has specific requirements, but an experienced partner can handle eligibility assessment, visa and work permit applications, and documentation as in this case, where 35 work permits were secured with no delays.

items.question: Can one provider handle both immigration and payroll for Netherlands relocation?
items.answer: Yes. Integrated programs cover [immigration](/immigration) and work permits, [[EOR]] employment, and Dutch payroll and social security in one flow reducing coordination and ensuring employees are paid and compliant from day one.

caseStudies.title: Professional Services Firm Global HR Transformation
caseStudies.excerpt: A global professional services firm unified fragmented HR and [[payroll]] across multiple countries through our centralized outsourcing solution improved accuracy, [[compliance]], and operational efficiency.
caseStudies.testimonial: Our international HR and [[payroll]] were all over the place. Jackson & Frank brought everything into one streamlined operation so we have clear visibility, consistent processes, and can focus on what we do best: serving our clients.

background.title: Overview
background.content: A global professional services firm was running HR and payroll in multiple countries through disconnected systems and processes. They wanted one partner to centralize operations, improve accuracy, and reduce compliance risk while they focused on client delivery.

problem.title: Client challenge
problem.content: International growth had left them with separate payroll systems, inconsistent compliance, and no single view of global HR. Internal teams were overloaded, compliance risk was rising, and scaling the workforce was getting harder. They needed global standardization without losing local accuracy.

solution.title: Our solution
solution.content: We built a single multi-country workforce model: consolidated payroll, compliance and regulatory oversight, HR administration and lifecycle management, and centralized reporting. We unified their HR and payroll so they could scale with clarity and control.

results.label: Global HR & payroll
results.description: One centralized HR and payroll operation across countries.

results.label: Payroll accuracy
results.description: Higher accuracy and faster payroll processing.

results.label: Regional practices
results.description: Standardized processes across all regions.

results.label: Admin workload
results.description: Lower administrative load on internal teams.

fullContent.executiveSummary: A global professional services firm was struggling with fragmented HR and [payroll](/payroll) across multiple countries different systems, inconsistent processes, and heavy admin load. We implemented a centralized multi-country workforce model so they could run HR and [[payroll]] from one place, with better accuracy and lower risk, and focus on clients.

businessChallenge.intro: Expansion had created real operational strain:
bullets: Disconnected payroll in multiple countries
Inconsistent [[compliance]] with local labor laws
No central HR oversight or reporting
Admin overload and rising [[compliance]] risk
Difficulty scaling the global workforce
businessChallenge.closing: They needed a partner to standardize globally while keeping local [compliance](/compliance) right.

fullContent.solutionIntro: We designed and implemented a customized multi-country workforce model for their footprint.

keyServices.title: 1. Global payroll consolidation
items: Standardized cycles and local tax compliance

steps: Phase 1 – Assessment: Existing payroll, compliance gaps, and HR workflows
Phase 2 – Standardization: Global policies and unified frameworks
Phase 3 – Integration: Migration into one management structure
Phase 4 – Optimization: Monitoring and process refinement
implementationApproach.closing: This delivered a single, scalable operating model.

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions about global HR and payroll transformation
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about consolidating HR and payroll for professional services firms

items.question: How do you unify fragmented HR and payroll across multiple countries?
items.answer: A phased approach typically includes assessment of existing [payroll](/payroll) and [compliance](/compliance) gaps, standardization of global policies, integration into one management structure, and ongoing optimization. This delivers a single, scalable operating model with improved accuracy and lower admin load.

items.question: What are the benefits of global payroll consolidation?
items.answer: Consolidated [payroll](/payroll) gives you one view of global HR and [[payroll]], higher accuracy, consistent regional practices, and reduced administrative burden so your team can focus on client delivery instead of disconnected systems.

items.question: Can global payroll outsourcing improve compliance?
items.answer: Yes. A dedicated partner monitors employment and labor law, statutory reporting, and contract standards across countries reducing compliance risk and keeping practices aligned with local regulations.

items.question: What does HR administration and reporting include in a global setup?
items.answer: Typically: onboarding, documentation, and lifecycle management; centralized reporting and visibility; and workforce and cost analytics so you have one place for HR data and decisions across regions.

items.question: Is global HR transformation suitable for professional services firms?
items.answer: Yes. Professional services firms with international growth often face disconnected payroll, inconsistent compliance, and heavy admin. A centralized multi-country workforce model can standardize operations while preserving local accuracy and reducing risk.

caseStudies.title: Engineering & Geoscience Company European Talent Expansion
caseStudies.excerpt: Hired and onboarded 19 non-EU professionals across [[Netherlands]], Czechia, and [[Germany]] with no local entities zero delays.
caseStudies.testimonial: We needed 19 non-EU specialists in three countries and had no entities there. Jackson & Frank became the employer, handled visas and [[payroll]], and managed all requirements. We could concentrate on delivering projects instead of legal and admin.

background.title: Overview
background.content: A global engineering and geoscience firm needed to grow its European team quickly with specialized non-EU talent. They had no entities in the target countries and needed a way to hire and employ people in the Netherlands, Czechia, and Germany without setting up locally.

problem.title: Client challenge
problem.content: Their European growth depended on hiring niche talent geoscientists, structural engineers, field specialists often from outside the EU. They had no local entities, faced complex immigration and differing employment laws, and had to run payroll in three countries. Delays would put project delivery at risk.

solution.title: Our solution
solution.content: We ran a structured international hiring program: we acted as legal employer in the Netherlands, Czechia, and Germany; handled work permits and visas; set up multi-country payroll; and supported fast onboarding. We hired 19 non-EU professionals with no legal or immigration delays.

results.label: Non-EU professionals
results.description: Hired and employed across three European countries.

results.label: Countries
results.description: Netherlands, Czechia, and Germany covered at once.

results.label: Jurisdictions
results.description: Immigration, labor, and payroll requirements met.

results.label: Legal delays
results.description: No legal or immigration delays; faster project staffing.

fullContent.executiveSummary: A global engineering and geoscience firm needed to hire 19 specialized non-EU professionals in Europe quickly. With no entities in the Netherlands, Czechia, or Germany, they needed one partner for employment, immigration, and payroll. We acted as EOR in all three countries, managed work permits and visas, and onboarded everyone with no delays.

businessChallenge.intro: Hiring non-EU talent in Europe meant:
bullets: No legal entities in the target countries
Complex EU immigration and work permits
Different employment and [[payroll]] rules in each country
Tight project deadlines delays would hit delivery
businessChallenge.closing: They needed a partner that could employ people legally and run [payroll](/payroll) in three countries at once.

fullContent.solutionIntro: We deployed a structured international employment framework and acted as EOR in all three countries.

items: Labor law and visa eligibility review per country

items: Legal employer in Netherlands, Czechia, and Germany

Phase 2: EOR and legal employment framework setup
Phase 3: Hiring, contracts, permits, and onboarding

faqs.title: Frequently asked questions about hiring non-EU talent in Europe
faqs.subtitle: Common questions about EOR and immigration for engineering and technical talent

items.question: Can I hire non-EU professionals in Europe without a local entity?
items.answer: Yes. An Employer of Record (EOR) can act as the legal employer in the Netherlands, Czechia, Germany, and other European countries while work permits and visas are handled. In this case, 19 non-EU professionals were hired across three countries with zero legal delays.

items.question: How do work permits work for non-EU hires in the Netherlands, Czechia, and Germany?
items.answer: Each country has its own immigration rules. An EOR partner typically handles eligibility, work permit and visa applications, and documentation so your team can focus on project delivery as in this engineering and geoscience case study.

items.question: What is EOR for international hiring in Europe?
items.answer: Employer of Record (EOR) means a local entity becomes the legal employer: they handle contracts, benefits, payroll, and compliance in each country so you can hire without setting up your own entities in the Netherlands, Czechia, Germany, or elsewhere.

items.question: How long does it take to hire non-EU talent in multiple European countries?
items.answer: Timelines depend on country and role. In this case study, 19 non-EU professionals were hired and onboarded across the Netherlands, Czechia, and Germany in a phased rollout with no legal or immigration delays.

items.question: Can you run payroll for non-EU employees in multiple European jurisdictions?
items.answer: Yes. Multi-country payroll covers tax, social security, and statutory requirements in each country. With EOR and immigration handled by the same partner, payroll and onboarding can run in parallel for faster project staffing.

items.question: How fast can you set up a workforce in the Netherlands?

items.question: What does EOR in the Netherlands include?
items.answer: Typically: legal employer for the Dutch workforce, Dutch-law contracts and benefits, statutory filings and labor requirements, Dutch [payroll](/payroll) and tax and social security, and onboarding and ongoing HR support so you stay compliant without a local entity.

items.question: Is Dutch payroll complex for international companies?

An [[EOR]] handles payroll, payslips, and reporting so you avoid errors and [compliance](/compliance) issues as in this smart mobility [case study](/case-studies) where 10 employees were managed with zero [[payroll]] errors.
